name: 2p15_p16_1_microdeletion_BCL11A_REL
description: 2p15-p16.1 microdeletion syndrome — ~1.5-5 Mb deletions causing ID, microcephaly, autism, optic nerve hypoplasia, and characteristic facies. Critical genes BCL11A (HbF regulation, ID) and REL. Most breakpoints are non-recurrent (MMBIR), but flanking SD blocks at 2p15 and 2p16.1 mediate a fraction of recurrent events. Complements the existing chr2_2p15_p16_polymicrogyria_BPs entry with the broader microdeletion phenotype.

architecture.internal_structure: Flanking SD blocks of ~10-50 kb at >95% identity (low SD density region); unique core contains BCL11A (primary dosage gene; persistence of HbF when LoF), REL (NF-kB family), USP34, XPO1, COMMD1, FANCL, PUS10, PEX13.

related_diseases: 2p15-p16.1 microdeletion syndrome (ID, microcephaly, optic nerve hypoplasia)
persistence of fetal hemoglobin (HPFH) via BCL11A
